epic_title: Conflict Coach v1 — Full-Stack AI Mediation Web App
planning_assumptions: The invitee's pre-join display name (typed by initiator at case creation as 'What's their name?') is not in the spec's cases/create mutation input. We add inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()) to the cases table and pass it through cases/create.
Solo mode isSolo=true pre-binds the same userId to both initiator and invitee slots; partyStates has two rows for the same userId with different roles. The party toggle filters by role, not userId.
The spec's COST_LIMITED status is not defined in the schema's case status union. It is added to cases.status in T2.
cases.tokenCount: v.optional(v.number()) is added to the cases table in T2 for AI cost tracking.
Convex Auth is the chosen auth provider (TQ1). The Clerk swap path is documented in README but not implemented.
Admin role promotion is a manual Convex Dashboard action in v1; no self-serve admin claim UI is built.
Typing indicators ('Jordan is typing...') reserve visual space per the design doc but Convex Presence logic is deferred to v1.1 per tech spec §8.3.
The decline-invite path sets the case to CLOSED_ABANDONED per design doc §4.3; added to state machine in T8.
The /cases/:id/invite post-create screen is a distinct route; invitee display name comes from inviteeName.
P2 user stories (US-16 email notifications, US-17 voluntary sharing, US-18 PDF export, US-19 inline rephrase) are explicitly out of scope.
ToS and privacy policy are static placeholder /terms and /privacy routes, not full tasks; linked from auth screens and case creation.

Cost tracking: tokenCount is accumulated per AI action call. Soft cap $2 ≈ 600k Sonnet tokens (hardcoded pricing constant); hard cap $10. Pricing constant is isolated in a single config file for easy update.

T17 budget enforcement logic and error-handling hardening is designed to run after T12-T15 as a cross-cutting enhancement. Token accumulation ACs are embedded in T12-T15 themselves; T17 adds the cap enforcement, degradation behavior, and Anthropic error handling.
dependency_graph_notes: Four strata: (1) Infrastructure [T1-T2], (2) Core modules [T3-T11]: auth, authorization, privacy filter, prompts, compression, state machine are parallel after T2+T4; templates backend (T11) unblocks case creation (T9); (3) Feature backends [T12-T17]: private coaching → synthesis → joint chat entry mutation (T14a) → joint chat coach (T14b) → draft coach; closure parallel after T14a; cost tracking (T17) is a cross-cutting hardening pass after T12-T15; (4) Frontend [T18-T32]: design tokens + shared components (T19a, T19b) → all views. T33 (accessibility) and T34 (E2E) both depend on T30+T32 (all feature UI complete) but NOT on each other — they can run in parallel. T35 (unit tests) depends only on T5+T6+T7+T8 and can run very early. T36 (CI) waits for T33+T34+T35.

tasks.title: Project scaffolding and cold-start setup
tasks.summary: Initialize the project with Vite + React 18 + TypeScript, Tailwind v3, shadcn/ui, React Router v6, Convex client, ESLint + Prettier. Create .env.example, .gitignore, and README with the exact cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3. Verify `git clone && npm install && npx convex dev && npm run dev` produces a running empty React app.
acceptance_criteria: `npm install` completes without errors on a clean checkout
`npx convex dev` starts without errors (Convex project configured)
`npm run dev` serves a React app at localhost:5173 with no console errors
ESLint and Prettier configs present; `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` pass on the empty project
`tsc --noEmit` passes with strict mode enabled
README contains verbatim the cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3
.env.example includes all env vars from §11.2: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MOCK, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, SITE_URL, MAGIC_LINK_EMAIL_FROM, VITE_CONVEX_URL, VITE_SITE_URL

tasks.title: Convex schema definition (all tables, all fields)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/schema.ts exactly as specified in tech spec §3.1 plus planning-assumption additions. All 10 tables: users, cases, partyStates, privateMessages, jointMessages, draftSessions, draftMessages, inviteTokens, templates, templateVersions, auditLog. Cases table includes: COST_LIMITED in status union, inviteeName, tokenCount. Document all 6 key invariants as comments.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 tables match tech spec §3.1 field names, types, and union literals exactly

cases.status union includes COST_LIMITED in addition to the 7 spec values: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING, BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING, READY_FOR_JOINT, JOINT_ACTIVE, CLOSED_RESOLVED, CLOSED_UNRESOLVED, CLOSED_ABANDONED
cases table includes: inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()), tokenCount: v.optional(v.number())
partyStates table includes: closureProposalSummary: v.optional(v.string()) for the closure proposal flow
All indexes present: by_email, by_initiator, by_invitee, by_case, by_case_and_user, by_token, by_template, by_actor, by_draft_session, by_case (on jointMessages, privateMessages, draftMessages)
`npx convex dev` applies the schema without errors
All 6 key invariants from §3.2 documented as inline comments
`tsc --noEmit` passes

tasks.title: Authentication — Convex Auth with magic link and Google OAuth
tasks.summary: Configure Convex Auth with email magic link and Google OAuth. On first login, upsert a users row (email, displayName from provider, role: 'USER'). Session persists across reloads until explicit logout or 30-day expiry. Wire ConvexAuthProvider and ConvexProvider into main.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: User registers and logs in via magic link: email submission sends a link; clicking the link establishes a session
User logs in via Google OAuth: OAuth flow completes and establishes a session
First login via either provider creates a users row with role='USER' and correct email
Subsequent logins upsert (do not duplicate) the users row
Session persists across browser reload (reactive query confirms identity)
Session expires after 30 days of inactivity
ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() returns the identity in any Convex function after login

tasks.title: Authorization helper module and ConvexError codes
tasks.summary: Create convex/lib/auth.ts with requireAuth(ctx) helper, assertPartyToCase, assertAdmin. Define all 9 ConvexError codes from tech spec §7.4. Create convex/lib/pricing.ts with ANTHROPIC_PRICING constant (soft cap $2, hard cap $10) as an isolated config — one place to update when pricing changes.
acceptance_criteria: requireAuth(ctx) returns { identity, user } or throws ConvexError({ code: 'UNAUTHENTICATED', httpStatus: 401 })
All 9 error codes typed as constants: UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, INVALID_INPUT, TOKEN_INVALID, RATE_LIMITED, AI_ERROR, INTERNAL
ConvexError shape: { code: string, message: string, httpStatus: number }
assertPartyToCase throws FORBIDDEN if caller is neither initiatorUserId nor inviteeUserId
assertAdmin throws FORBIDDEN if user.role !== 'ADMIN'
convex/lib/pricing.ts exports: SOFT_CAP_USD = 2.0, HARD_CAP_USD = 10.0, SONNET_INPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, SONNET_OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, HAIKU_INPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, HAIKU_OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN
All subsequent Convex functions import error codes from convex/lib/auth.ts consistently

tasks.title: Privacy response filter module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/privacyFilter.ts: given a candidate AI response string and a set of other-party message strings, detect any ≥8 consecutive whitespace-token substring from the other-party messages appearing verbatim (case-insensitive) in the response. Returns { passed: boolean, matchedSubstring?: string }.
acceptance_criteria: filterResponse(response: string, otherPartyMessages: string[]): { passed: boolean, matchedSubstring?: string }
passed=false when any 8+ consecutive token window from other-party messages appears verbatim in response (case-insensitive)
passed=true when no such match exists
Handles empty otherPartyMessages → passed=true
Handles empty response → passed=true
Unit test: exact 10-word phrase from other party in response → passed=false
Unit test: response rephrases → passed=true
Unit test: 7-token substring (below threshold) → passed=true
Unit test: exact 8-token substring → passed=false (boundary case)
Performance: 10k-word message set processed without timeout in Vitest

tasks.title: Prompt assembly module for all four AI roles
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/prompts.ts with assemblePrompt(opts) for PRIVATE_COACH, SYNTHESIS, COACH, DRAFT_COACH. Each returns { system: string, messages: Message[] }. All system prompts include explicit no-quote rules and crisis safety language. PRIVATE_COACH includes keyword-level crisis detection instructions: if user message contains crisis signals, the AI response should include a crisis resources notice.
acceptance_criteria: assemblePrompt({ role: 'PRIVATE_COACH' }) system prompt matches tech spec §6.3.1 verbatim plus crisis guardrail: 'If the user's message contains signals of immediate danger, abuse, or suicidal ideation, provide a crisis resource statement (e.g., "If you're in immediate danger, please contact emergency services or a crisis hotline") before continuing.'
assemblePrompt({ role: 'SYNTHESIS' }) produces a prompt requiring strict { forInitiator: string, forInvitee: string } JSON per §6.3.2, with verbatim critical instruction from §6.3.2
assemblePrompt({ role: 'COACH' }) incorporates templateVersion.globalGuidance + coachInstructions when provided; falls back to baseline when not
assemblePrompt({ role: 'DRAFT_COACH' }) incorporates draftCoachInstructions; explicitly excludes other party's synthesis and private content from context
All four system prompts contain: 'NEVER quote, closely paraphrase, or otherwise surface the other party's raw words'
Function is pure TypeScript — no DB calls, no SDK calls; testable in Vitest
Unit test: SYNTHESIS system string contains verbatim critical instruction from §6.3.2
Unit test: DRAFT_COACH context assembly does not include other party's synthesis text even when provided in opts

tasks.title: Transcript compression module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/transcriptCompression.ts: when token count exceeds budget (60k for user-facing, 10k for classification), take oldest 50% of messages, call Claude Haiku with compression prompt, replace with SUMMARY message. Cache by content hash.
acceptance_criteria: compressIfNeeded(messages, budgetTokens, anthropic): returns original array when under budget without any API call
When over budget: oldest 50% replaced by { role: 'system', content: 'SUMMARY: ...' } from Haiku
Compression prompt calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001: 'Summarize this conversation segment in 500 tokens or fewer, preserving facts, decisions, emotional tone, and unresolved threads.'
Content hash caching: same message set compressed twice makes only one Haiku API call
Token counting uses word-count approximation (4 chars ≈ 1 token)
Unit test: array exceeding 60k budget is reduced to a smaller set containing one SUMMARY message
Unit test: array under budget returned unchanged; API not called

tasks.title: Case state machine module (all transitions including decline-invite)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/stateMachine.ts with pure transition functions for all case states. Covered transitions: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING (invitee joins), DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→CLOSED_ABANDONED (invitee declines), BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING→READY_FOR_JOINT (both complete PC + synthesis done), READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE (first party enters), JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_RESOLVED, JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_UNRESOLVED, JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_ABANDONED (cron), any→COST_LIMITED.
acceptance_criteria: Each transition function is pure: takes current case state + relevant party states, returns new status or throws ConvexError CONFLICT
DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→CLOSED_ABANDONED: triggered by invitee declining; explicitly tested
BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING→READY_FOR_JOINT: requires both partyStates.privateCoachingCompletedAt set AND both synthesisTexts populated
READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE: any party can trigger; idempotent if already JOINT_ACTIVE
JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_RESOLVED: requires other party's closureProposed=true
Sending a joint message when case is not JOINT_ACTIVE throws CONFLICT
All pure TypeScript, no DB calls — fully unit-testable
Unit test: all 9 valid transitions succeed from correct precondition state
Unit test: 8+ illegal transitions (wrong source state) each throw CONFLICT
Unit test: marking PC complete twice is idempotent (no error, state unchanged)
Unit test: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→CLOSED_ABANDONED (decline) succeeds and returns CLOSED_ABANDONED

tasks.title: Case CRUD backend (create, get, list, partyStates, updateMyForm)
tasks.summary: Implement Convex queries and mutations for case management: cases/create (generates crypto-random invite token, pins templateVersionId, creates partyStates for initiator, handles solo mode), cases/get, cases/list, cases/partyStates (self + other-party phase-only), cases/updateMyForm. All enforce auth + party-membership rules.
acceptance_criteria: cases/create: creates cases row with status=DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING, isSolo, category, templateVersionId (current version of selected template), initiatorUserId, inviteeName, tokenCount=0, createdAt, updatedAt
cases/create: creates partyStates row for initiator with role=INITIATOR
cases/create: inserts inviteTokens row with 32-char crypto-random url-safe token, status=ACTIVE; returns caseId + invite URL as SITE_URL + /invite/ + token
cases/create with isSolo=true: pre-binds invitee to same userId with role=INVITEE; creates second partyStates row (role=INVITEE); sets case.status=BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING
cases/list: returns only cases where caller is initiatorUserId or inviteeUserId
cases/get: returns NOT_FOUND if caller is not a party
cases/partyStates: returns { self: full PartyState, other: { role, formCompletedAt: boolean, privateCoachingCompletedAt: boolean } } — no raw text from other party
cases/updateMyForm: updates caller's partyStates form fields; throws FORBIDDEN if not the party
Token stored as plaintext per §12 note

tasks.title: Invite flow backend (redeem mutation, preview query, token validation)
tasks.summary: Implement invites/redeem mutation (atomic: validate token, bind invitee, create partyStates, mark token CONSUMED, advance case to BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING) and invites/preview query (returns category + initiatorDisplayName + mainTopic + inviteeName without private content).
acceptance_criteria: invites/redeem: reads inviteTokens by token field; throws TOKEN_INVALID if status != ACTIVE

invites/redeem: atomically — sets cases.inviteeUserId, creates partyStates row for invitee (role=INVITEE), sets inviteTokens.status=CONSUMED + consumedAt + consumedByUserId, advances case to BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING
invites/redeem: throws CONFLICT if caller is already the initiator (self-invite prevention in non-solo mode)
invites/redeem on a CONSUMED token: throws TOKEN_INVALID
invites/preview: returns { category, initiatorDisplayName, mainTopic, inviteeName } with NO private coaching content (no description, no desiredOutcome, no synthesisText)
invites/preview on non-existent token: returns null (not NOT_FOUND — allow UI to render appropriate state)
End-to-end test: create case → get token → redeem as different user → case.inviteeUserId is set, token.status=CONSUMED

tasks.title: Templates backend and seed data
tasks.summary: Implement template management queries and mutations: templates/list, admin/templates/listAll, admin/templateVersions/list, admin/templates/create, admin/templates/publishNewVersion, admin/templates/archive. All admin mutations write to auditLog and call assertAdmin. Create convex/seed.ts with one admin user and three default templates.
acceptance_criteria: templates/list: returns only non-archived templates; optionally filtered by category
admin/templates/create: creates templates + initial templateVersions row; sets currentVersionId; writes TEMPLATE_CREATED to auditLog
admin/templates/publishNewVersion: inserts new (immutable) templateVersions row; updates currentVersionId; version monotonically increments; writes TEMPLATE_VERSION_PUBLISHED to auditLog
admin/templates/archive: sets archivedAt; existing cases with pinned templateVersionId are NOT affected; writes TEMPLATE_ARCHIVED to auditLog
All admin mutations throw FORBIDDEN for non-ADMIN users
convex/seed.ts: creates one user with role=ADMIN and three template+version rows (workplace, family, personal) each with minimal globalGuidance; throws INTERNAL if called outside dev environment
templateVersions rows have no update mutation (immutability enforced by absence)

tasks.title: Private coaching backend (send message, AI response, mark complete, crisis detection, token tracking)
tasks.summary: Implement: privateCoaching/myMessages query (caller's messages only), privateCoaching/sendUserMessage mutation (inserts user message, checks for crisis keywords, schedules generateAIResponse), generateAIResponse action (assembles PRIVATE_COACH prompt via T6 + compression via T7, calls Claude Sonnet with streaming, updates privateMessages row, increments cases.tokenCount), privateCoaching/markComplete mutation (schedules synthesis if both complete).
acceptance_criteria: privateCoaching/myMessages: returns only rows where userId == caller; FORBIDDEN if caller is not party
privateCoaching/myMessages: the privateMessages.by_case index is never exposed via any client-callable query

privateCoaching/sendUserMessage: inserts user message row with status=COMPLETE; checks message content against crisis keyword list (abuse, suicidal, emergency, immediate danger, restraining order); if crisis keywords found, schedules a crisis-flag side-effect that causes the AI response to include a crisis resources notice
generateAIResponse: assembles PRIVATE_COACH prompt including caller's form fields + prior private message history ONLY (no other party data); applies compression via T7 at 60k token budget
generateAIResponse: inserts STREAMING message row before API call; updates content in batches every ~50ms of tokens; sets COMPLETE on finish; sets ERROR on failure
generateAIResponse: after completion, calls a mutation to increment cases.tokenCount by (usage.input_tokens + usage.output_tokens)
generateAIResponse: if cases.tokenCount × pricing >= HARD_CAP_USD, returns early with a system message 'AI assistance is currently limited for this case' without calling Claude
privateCoaching/markComplete: sets partyStates.privateCoachingCompletedAt; idempotent if already set
privateCoaching/markComplete: if both parties have privateCoachingCompletedAt set, schedules synthesis/generate action
Security test: calling privateCoaching/myMessages as User B for User A's caseId+userId returns FORBIDDEN

tasks.title: AI synthesis backend (generate action, privacy filter, state transition)
tasks.summary: Implement synthesis/generate action: reads both parties' private messages (server-side), assembles SYNTHESIS prompt via T6, calls Claude Sonnet (non-streaming), validates JSON output { forInitiator, forInvitee }, runs T5 privacy filter on each half (up to 2 retries; on final failure uses generic fallback + flags in auditLog), writes synthesisText + synthesisGeneratedAt to both partyStates, advances case to READY_FOR_JOINT. Increments tokenCount.
acceptance_criteria: synthesis/generate reads privateMessages for BOTH parties via by_case index (server-side only)
Calls claude-sonnet-4-5 with SYNTHESIS prompt from T6; validates response is { forInitiator: string, forInvitee: string } JSON
If response is not valid JSON: retries once; second failure throws AI_ERROR
Runs T5 filterResponse on forInitiator (with invitee's private messages as 'other party') and on forInvitee (with initiator's messages as 'other party')

Privacy filter failure: retries generation up to 2 times; third failure writes SYNTHESIS_FILTER_FAILED to auditLog + uses generic fallback: 'Your Coach has prepared some general guidance for this session...'
On success: writes synthesisText + synthesisGeneratedAt to both partyStates rows atomically; sets case.status=READY_FOR_JOINT; sets updatedAt
Increments cases.tokenCount by usage tokens
Idempotent: if synthesisText already populated, returns without re-generating
Does not check HARD_CAP (synthesis is generated once per case; it is a gate, not a recurring call)

tasks.title: Joint chat entry mutation and Coach facilitation backend
tasks.summary: Implement jointChat/enter mutation (READY_FOR_JOINT → JOINT_ACTIVE transition, schedules opening Coach message), jointChat/messages query, jointChat/mySynthesis query, jointChat/sendUserMessage mutation (inserts user message, schedules generateCoachResponse with triggerType='USER_MESSAGE'). Implement generateCoachResponse action: Haiku classification gate (INFLAMMATORY / PROGRESS / QUESTION_TO_COACH / NORMAL_EXCHANGE) → conditionally calls Sonnet + T5 filter; handles MENTION and TIMER trigger types. Increments tokenCount.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/enter: throws CONFLICT if case.status is not READY_FOR_JOINT (and not already JOINT_ACTIVE); atomically sets case.status=JOINT_ACTIVE + updatedAt; schedules generateCoachResponse with triggerType='OPENING'
jointChat/enter is idempotent: if case is already JOINT_ACTIVE, returns success without error

generateCoachResponse with triggerType='OPENING': calls Sonnet to generate an opening message grounded in case.category + the mainTopics of both parties (from their partyStates); inserts as COACH jointMessage
jointChat/messages: throws FORBIDDEN if caller is not party; throws CONFLICT if case status is DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING or BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING

jointChat/sendUserMessage input schema: { caseId: Id<'cases'>, content: string } — no triggerType accepted from client. @-mention detection is performed inside the mutation body (content.toLowerCase().includes('@coach')); if detected, schedules generateCoachResponse with triggerType='MENTION'; otherwise triggerType='USER_MESSAGE'. Throws CONFLICT if case.status is not JOINT_ACTIVE; inserts user joint message row.

generateCoachResponse with triggerType='USER_MESSAGE': calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to classify last user message; Coach responds only for INFLAMMATORY, PROGRESS, QUESTION_TO_COACH — skips for NORMAL_EXCHANGE
Classification uses 10k token budget with compression via T7
generateCoachResponse with triggerType='MENTION': always generates Sonnet response

generateCoachResponse with triggerType='TIMER': fires when 5+ user exchanges have occurred since the last COACH jointMessage; always generates; checked synchronously in sendUserMessage before scheduling
Sonnet context: joint chat history + BOTH parties' synthesisTexts (NOT raw private messages); compressed via T7 at 60k budget
T5 privacy filter applied to Coach Sonnet response; on filter failure → retry up to 2x → generic fallback 'I'm having trouble responding to that right now. Could either of you rephrase?'
INFLAMMATORY-triggered responses set isIntervention=true on the jointMessage row
generateCoachResponse increments cases.tokenCount by usage tokens; enforces HARD_CAP (skips Sonnet call if at cap, posts boilerplate)
jointChat/mySynthesis: returns only the caller's own synthesisText

tasks.title: Draft Coach backend (session lifecycle, AI conversation, readiness detection, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement draftCoach/session query, draftCoach/startSession mutation, draftCoach/sendMessage mutation, generateResponse action (readiness detection, finalDraft production), draftCoach/sendFinalDraft mutation, draftCoach/discardSession mutation. Increments tokenCount.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/startSession: creates draftSessions row with status=ACTIVE; throws CONFLICT if case not JOINT_ACTIVE
draftCoach/sendMessage: inserts draftMessages row for caller; schedules generateResponse
generateResponse: context = drafting user's joint chat history + their own synthesisText ONLY (no other party synthesis, no other party private messages)

Readiness detection: if user message matches (case-insensitive) any of ['i\'m ready', 'draft it', 'write the message', 'generate draft', 'looks good, write it'] OR a canonical button message → produce { 'draft': '...' } structured JSON response; writes result to draftSessions.finalDraft
Non-readiness turns: stream conversational response into draftMessages row (STREAMING → COMPLETE)
draftCoach/sendFinalDraft: reads session.finalDraft; throws CONFLICT if finalDraft is null; internally calls jointChat/sendUserMessage with finalDraft content; marks session status=SENT
draftCoach/discardSession: sets status=DISCARDED; no message posted to joint chat
draftCoach/session: returns only if caller is session owner; FORBIDDEN otherwise
Draft Coach conversation is invisible to the other party (never appears in jointMessages queries)
generateResponse increments cases.tokenCount; skips Sonnet call if at HARD_CAP

tasks.title: Case closure backend (propose, confirm, reject, unilateral close, abandoned cron)
tasks.summary: Implement closure mutations: jointChat/proposeClosure, jointChat/confirmClosure, jointChat/rejectClosure, jointChat/unilateralClose. Implement convex/crons.ts daily job for CLOSED_ABANDONED detection. All write to auditLog.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/proposeClosure: sets partyStates.closureProposed=true for caller; writes proposed summary to partyStates.closureProposalSummary; throws CONFLICT if case not JOINT_ACTIVE; writes CLOSURE_PROPOSED to auditLog

jointChat/confirmClosure: throws CONFLICT if other party has not set closureProposed=true; atomically sets case.status=CLOSED_RESOLVED, case.closedAt, case.closureSummary (from proposer's closureProposalSummary), case.updatedAt; clears closureProposed on both parties; writes CASE_CLOSED_RESOLVED to auditLog
jointChat/rejectClosure: clears proposer's closureProposed and closureProposalSummary; optionally posts a system jointMessage notifying the proposer; writes CLOSURE_REJECTED to auditLog
jointChat/unilateralClose: sets case.status=CLOSED_UNRESOLVED + closedAt + updatedAt; writes CASE_CLOSED_UNRESOLVED to auditLog
Closed cases (any CLOSED_* or COST_LIMITED) make sendUserMessage, proposeClosure, confirmClosure throw CONFLICT
Cron job in convex/crons.ts: runs daily; queries JOINT_ACTIVE cases where max(jointMessages.createdAt) < now - 30 days; sets each to CLOSED_ABANDONED + closedAt; writes CASE_ABANDONED to auditLog
Dashboard query still returns CLOSED_* cases for archival access
closureProposalSummary field is defined in T2's schema (partyStates table); T16 uses it without any further schema changes needed

tasks.title: AI cost tracking hardening: budget enforcement, soft-cap degradation, Anthropic error handling
tasks.summary: Cross-cutting hardening pass after T12-T15: implement budget enforcement in all AI actions (token accumulation already added in T12-T15; this task adds soft-cap degradation behavior, hard-cap enforcement, and full Anthropic error handling). Verify caps work end-to-end. Does not re-open T12-T15 for token counting (already there), but adds cap-enforcement logic and tests.
acceptance_criteria: Soft cap ($2): when cases.tokenCount × pricing >= SOFT_CAP_USD, generateCoachResponse (T14) posts a templated boilerplate message instead of calling Sonnet: 'I\'m limited right now — consider summarizing where you are and whether you\'ve reached agreement.' Private coaching (T12) and synthesis (T13) continue unaffected

Hard cap ($10): when cases.tokenCount × pricing >= HARD_CAP_USD, all four AI actions (generateAIResponse, synthesis/generate, generateCoachResponse, generateResponse) return early without calling Claude; each posts a system message explaining the limitation; case.status is set to COST_LIMITED in a mutation
On 429 from Anthropic: action waits 2000ms then retries exactly once; on second 429, sets message status=ERROR; throws RATE_LIMITED
On network timeout (>30s): sets message status=ERROR with content 'Coach timed out. You can retry.'; throws AI_ERROR
On 401/403 from Anthropic: sets message status=ERROR with 'Coach is unavailable'; writes API_KEY_ERROR event to auditLog; throws AI_ERROR
On Anthropic content filter: sets message status=ERROR with 'Coach can\'t respond to that — try rephrasing'; throws AI_ERROR
Integration test: seed a case with tokenCount at the soft cap threshold; send a joint message; verify Coach posts boilerplate (not a Sonnet call)
Integration test: seed a case with tokenCount at the hard cap; verify all AI actions return without API call and status becomes COST_LIMITED

tasks.title: Design tokens and global styles
tasks.summary: Implement all CSS custom properties from design doc §2.1, Tailwind config mapping, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts, dark mode via prefers-color-scheme, shadow levels, and motion tokens. Reduced-motion media query globally disables all animations.
acceptance_criteria: All 16 color tokens from §2.1 defined as CSS custom properties in src/index.css
Dark mode via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) with inverted luminance, same warm/low-saturation palette

Tailwind config maps each token to a semantic utility class: bg-canvas, bg-surface, bg-surface-subtle, text-primary, text-secondary, text-tertiary, accent, accent-hover, accent-subtle, danger, danger-subtle, warning, border-default, border-strong, coach-accent, private-tint
Inter variable font loaded (400/500/600 weights); JetBrains Mono loaded
Body: 15px / 1.6 line-height; chat bubbles: 16px
Four shadow utilities: shadow-0 (flat), shadow-1, shadow-2, shadow-3 matching §2.4 values
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) global rule: all transitions set to 0ms, animations paused

tasks.title: Core chat UI components (ChatWindow, MessageBubble, StreamingIndicator, MessageInput)
tasks.summary: Build the foundational chat primitives used by all three chat UIs (private coaching, joint chat, draft coach). ChatWindow: scrollable message list + MessageInput; auto-scrolls to latest unless user has scrolled up. MessageBubble: USER/AI/COACH/SYSTEM variants with STREAMING/COMPLETE/ERROR states, streaming cursor, copy button, Retry button. StreamingIndicator: 500ms blinking cursor. MessageInput: textarea + Send; Shift-Enter newline; Send disabled while any STREAMING row exists.
acceptance_criteria: ChatWindow renders a scrollable list; auto-scrolls to latest unless user scroll position is > 100px above bottom
MessageBubble USER: right-aligned, bg-surface background, no icon
MessageBubble COACH/AI: left-aligned, accent-subtle background, Sparkles icon
MessageBubble STREAMING: renders content + blinking cursor (500ms interval); no copy button
MessageBubble COMPLETE: renders full content + copy button (copies text to clipboard) + timestamp visible on hover
MessageBubble ERROR: warning-tint background, error message text, Retry button that calls the provided onRetry callback
StreamingIndicator: thin vertical bar, 500ms blink; rendered as aria-hidden
MessageInput: Enter sends (calls onSend callback); Shift+Enter inserts newline; Send button disabled when isStreaming prop is true
All components accept className prop for layout override
No accessibility defects introduced: MessageInput textarea has a visible label or aria-label

tasks.title: Secondary UI components (PrivacyBanner, PartyAvatar, CoachIntervention, PhaseHeader, StatusPill, CrisisCard, skeletons)
tasks.summary: Build remaining shared components: PrivacyBanner (persistent lock icon + privacy modal), PartyAvatar (role-colored circles), CoachIntervention (⟡ glyph + coach-accent border), PhaseHeader (top nav phase display), StatusPill (glyph-based status), CrisisCard (crisis resource notice surfaced when crisis keywords detected in private coaching), skeleton loaders for dashboard and case detail.
acceptance_criteria: PrivacyBanner: persistent lock icon; clicking opens a Dialog explaining what's private and why (exact copy from design doc §4.7); reads 'Private conversation. Only you and the AI coach see this.' to screen readers
PartyAvatar: initiator=soft blue (#BFDBFE), invitee=soft rose (#FECDD3), coach=coach-accent lavender (#8B7AB5); shows first initial
CoachIntervention: 2px left border in coach-accent; ⟡ glyph prefix; accent-subtle background
PhaseHeader: top nav component showing current case phase label (Private Coaching / Joint Session / etc.) + Back to Dashboard link
StatusPill: renders ● (green), ○ (gray), ◐ (amber), ◼ (neutral) with correct color mapping per §4.4

CrisisCard: appears above MessageInput when crisis keywords detected in the user's most recent message; shows message 'If you are in immediate danger or experiencing abuse, please contact emergency services or a crisis hotline [link]. Conflict Coach is not a substitute for professional help.' Dismissable.
Dashboard skeleton: 3 case row skeletons (text placeholder blocks matching case row layout)
Case detail skeleton: matches the layout of the phase-appropriate view
Skeletons are shown for content loading > 300ms (implemented via a delay prop or CSS)

tasks.title: Landing page and login/register page
tasks.summary: Implement / (hero, 3-step explainer, privacy section, footer) and /login (email magic link, Google OAuth, magic-link-sent state, error state, ToS fine print). Wire Convex Auth signIn methods.
acceptance_criteria: / displays: single-sentence tagline, 3-step process explainer (Private Coaching → Shared Conversation → Resolution), privacy section with link to /privacy, footer with links to /terms, /privacy, contact
/ has a 'Start a case' CTA that routes to /login (logged out) or /dashboard (logged in); no testimonials, pricing, or repeated CTAs
/login: centered ~400px card; email input + 'Send magic link' primary button; 'or' divider; 'Continue with Google' secondary button
Submitting email triggers Convex Auth magic link; form replaced by 'Check your email — we sent a link to [email]'
Error state renders inline below the email input (not a toast)
Fine print links to /terms and /privacy; includes 'By signing in, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Policy.'
After successful auth, user redirected to /dashboard

Keyboard-only user can: Tab to email input → type → Tab to Send magic link → activate with Enter → Tab to Continue with Google → activate with Enter (verified by Playwright keyboard assertions in T34)

tasks.title: Invite acceptance page (/invite/:token)
tasks.summary: Implement invite acceptance per design doc §4.3. Logged-out: explanation + 'Sign in to continue' (token preserved through auth). Logged-in+unredeemed: show initiator name, mainTopic (labeled private), category, Accept/Decline. Decline → CLOSED_ABANDONED. Accept → invites/redeem → /cases/:id/private.
acceptance_criteria: GET /invite/:token by logged-out user: shows explanation card + 'Sign in to continue'; after auth user returns to /invite/:token (token preserved in URL)
Logged-in unredeemed view: shows initiator's displayName, mainTopic (labeled 'Alex wrote this in the shared summary. You\'ll have your own private space to share your perspective.'), category
Description, desiredOutcome, and private coaching content are NOT shown
Accept button calls invites/redeem; on success routes to /cases/:caseId/private
Decline button calls mutation → case.status=CLOSED_ABANDONED → redirect to /dashboard with toast 'You declined the invitation'
CONSUMED token shows: 'This invite has already been used.' + link to /dashboard
Non-existent token shows a NOT_FOUND error message

tasks.title: Dashboard page (/dashboard) with unilateral-close notification indicator
tasks.summary: Implement /dashboard per design doc §4.4. Active/Closed sections, case rows with status glyphs, Nudge button (>7 days no join), empty state, [+ New Case] button. Includes a notification indicator on case rows when the other party has unilaterally closed the case.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches cases/list; renders skeleton rows while loading
Active Cases section: all non-CLOSED_* cases; Closed Cases section: CLOSED_* cases, collapsed by default, auto-expands if a case just transitioned to CLOSED_* (reactive update)

Status glyph mapping: initiator waiting for invitee → ○ gray; user's turn to act → ● green; waiting on other party's PC → ○ gray; READY_FOR_JOINT → ◐ amber; JOINT_ACTIVE (new message) → ● green; JOINT_ACTIVE (no new message) → ○ gray; CLOSED_* → ◼

When case.status = CLOSED_UNRESOLVED and the caller is NOT the user who closed it: case row shows ◼ with a red dot indicator and the text 'Jordan closed this case.' The closed cases section auto-expands
Counterparty name: shows inviteeName before invitee joins, then invitee's displayName after join
Nudge button: appears on DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING cases where updatedAt > 7 days ago and inviteeUserId is null; clicking routes to /cases/:id/invite
Empty state: 'No cases yet. When you\'re ready to work through something, start a new case.' + link to /cases/new
[+ New Case] primary button routes to /cases/new
Case rows are keyboard-navigable; Enter activates the row

tasks.title: Case creation form and post-create invite sharing screen
tasks.summary: Implement /cases/new (5-step progressive form) and /cases/:id/invite (share screen). Form: category radio cards, main topic with 140-char counter + 'visible to other party' label, description + desired outcome with privacy lock icon + tooltip, other party's name, Advanced disclosure for solo mode. Crisis language on creation screen. Submit calls cases/create. Invite screen: large copyable link, email/text/copy options, 'What should I tell them?' collapsible.
acceptance_criteria: Five steps in sequence with Back/Next navigation; Submit only on step 5
Step 1: category as radio cards (not dropdown) for all 5 categories
Step 2: mainTopic with soft 140-char counter (allows over with visual warning); labeled 'visible to the other person'
Steps 3+4: description and desiredOutcome show lock icon with tooltip 'Only you and the AI coach will see this'; fields labeled as private
Step 5: other party name input; 'Advanced' disclosure with solo mode checkbox
Form displays prominently: 'Conflict Coach is not for crisis or abuse situations. If you are in immediate danger, please contact emergency services.'
Submit calls cases/create with all fields + correct templateVersionId from templates/list (category-matching current version); on success routes to /cases/:caseId/invite
/cases/:id/invite: invite URL in large monospace copyable field with Copy button
Email mailto pre-fills with template message from §4.6 verbatim
'Start your private coaching now' navigates to /cases/:caseId/private

tasks.title: Case detail router and phase-appropriate view scaffold
tasks.summary: Implement CaseDetail.tsx at /cases/:caseId routing to phase-appropriate sub-routes by case.status. Guard against phase-skipping. PhaseHeader shown on all sub-routes.
acceptance_criteria: GET /cases/:caseId redirects to the correct sub-route based on case.status
GET /cases/:caseId/private when case.status >= READY_FOR_JOINT: redirect to /cases/:caseId/ready with toast
GET /cases/:caseId/joint when case.status is DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING or BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING: redirect to /private with toast 'You can enter joint chat once both parties complete private coaching'
PhaseHeader component present on all case sub-routes with current phase label and Back-to-dashboard link
Loading: skeleton layout while cases/get is in flight
caseId not found or caller not a party: friendly error page (not blank screen)

tasks.title: Private coaching view (/cases/:id/private) with crisis card integration
tasks.summary: Implement PrivateCoachingView.tsx: full-height chat using ChatWindow (T19a), PrivacyBanner, mark-complete footer CTA + confirmation modal. Integrates CrisisCard (T19b) which appears above MessageInput when crisis keywords are detected in the user's message. AI integration via privateCoaching/myMessages + sendUserMessage + streaming.
acceptance_criteria: Loads privateCoaching/myMessages reactively; renders existing messages before user types
PrivacyBanner always visible at top; lock icon opens Dialog with privacy model explanation
Coach messages: left-aligned, accent-subtle, Sparkles icon; User messages: right-aligned, bg-surface
STREAMING → blinking cursor; COMPLETE → copy button + timestamp on hover; ERROR → Retry button
Send disabled while any STREAMING message exists; auto-scroll to latest unless user scrolled up
'Mark private coaching complete' is a subtle footer CTA (not a large primary button)
Mark-complete confirmation modal: shows message count; 'Continue Coaching' / 'Mark Complete' buttons
Confirm calls privateCoaching/markComplete; if case advances to READY_FOR_JOINT, routes to /cases/:id/ready
If other party not yet complete: stays on /private with note 'Waiting for [name] to finish their private coaching'

CrisisCard appears above MessageInput when the most recent user message contains crisis keywords (detected client-side from the message before sendUserMessage is called); card is dismissable but re-appears if a new crisis keyword message is sent

tasks.title: Ready-for-joint view (/cases/:id/ready, synthesis display, enter CTA)
tasks.summary: Implement ReadyForJointView.tsx: synthesis card with privacy label, markdown-rendered synthesis, 'Enter Joint Session' CTA that calls jointChat/enter (T14) and routes to /joint. Synthesis accessible from joint chat nav link.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches jointChat/mySynthesis; skeleton loader while in flight
Synthesis card shows privacy label: 'Private to you — [name] has their own version'
Synthesis text rendered as markdown (bold section headings, paragraph text)
'Enter Joint Session →' is a large primary button
Clicking calls jointChat/enter mutation; on success routes to /cases/:id/joint
jointChat/enter is idempotent: if case already JOINT_ACTIVE, routes to /joint without error
If the other party has already entered (case is JOINT_ACTIVE): shows 'Jordan has entered. Join them when you\'re ready.' + same CTA
If this party has not yet entered and case is still READY_FOR_JOINT: shows CTA without 'waiting' message

tasks.title: Joint chat view (/cases/:id/joint) with COST_LIMITED state handling
tasks.summary: Implement JointChatView.tsx: real-time reactive messages, party avatars, Coach ⟡ messages, @-mention, 'Coach is thinking' indicator, closure banner, 'My guidance' Sheet, closure modal trigger. Handles COST_LIMITED case status with banner and disabled Draft Coach.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/messages reactive query auto-updates both clients when new messages arrive
Party avatar colors: initiator=soft blue, invitee=soft rose, Coach=lavender; ⟡ glyph + coach-accent border on Coach messages
isIntervention=true messages: warning-tint (--warning) background
Timestamps on hover for each message
'Coach is thinking...' inline indicator when a Coach STREAMING message row is present
@-mention detection: if user input contains '@Coach' (case-insensitive), sendUserMessage adds triggerType='MENTION' metadata to schedule a guaranteed Coach response
Closure proposal banner: when other party has closureProposed=true, shows banner above input with proposed summary + 'Confirm' and 'Reject and keep talking' buttons
'My guidance' opens a Sheet/drawer showing the user's own synthesisText (read-only, markdown)
'Close' button in top nav opens the closure modal (implemented in T29)

COST_LIMITED state: when case.status = COST_LIMITED, a banner appears 'AI assistance is currently limited for this case'; Draft Coach button is hidden; user-to-user message send remains enabled; Coach messages area shows 'Coach is no longer available due to session limits'
Read-only mode: when case status is CLOSED_*, input and Draft Coach button are hidden; banner 'This case is closed. No new messages can be added.'
Mobile: Draft Coach rendered as a bottom Sheet (full-screen); top nav collapses

tasks.title: Draft Coach panel (side panel/bottom sheet, draft ready card, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement DraftCoachPanel.tsx: 420px right side panel on desktop, full-screen bottom Sheet on mobile. Private label, ChatWindow for draft conversation, 'Draft it for me' button, DraftReadyCard with Send/Edit/Refine/Discard actions. Strict send gate: Send is the only path to joint chat.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking '✨ Draft with Coach' calls draftCoach/startSession; opens panel
Panel shows '🔒 This is private to you. [Name] can\'t see what you\'re discussing here.'
Lock icon tooltip: 'Jordan can\'t see any of this. Only the final message you send goes to the joint chat.'
ChatWindow connects to draftCoach/sendMessage; shows conversation history from draftCoach/session
'Draft it for me' posts canonical 'Generate Draft' message; triggers readiness detection in backend
DraftReadyCard appears when draftSessions.finalDraft is populated: shows draft in bordered box
'Send this message' (PRIMARY): calls draftCoach/sendFinalDraft; panel closes; message appears in joint chat
'Edit before sending': transfers draft text to joint-chat main input; closes panel; does NOT auto-send
'Keep refining': returns to ChatWindow; AI can produce a new draft on next readiness signal
'Discard': calls draftCoach/discardSession; panel closes; no message sent
Esc closes panel; focus returns to joint-chat input
On mobile (<768px): renders as a full-screen Sheet from bottom
Focus moves to draft input textarea when panel opens

tasks.title: Closure flow UI and closed case view (/cases/:id/closed)
tasks.summary: Implement the closure modal (Resolved/Not resolved/Take a break), rejection flow, and /cases/:id/closed read-only archive view with Joint Chat / My Private Coaching / My Guidance tabs.
acceptance_criteria: Closure modal triggered by '[Close]' in joint chat top nav; three options: Resolved / Not resolved / Take a break
Resolved: summary textarea required (min 10 chars); 'Propose Resolution' calls jointChat/proposeClosure; modal closes; proposal banner appears in other party's view (reactive)
Not resolved: warning styling (danger-subtle background); calls jointChat/unilateralClose; case transitions to CLOSED_UNRESOLVED immediately for both parties
'Take a break': closes modal with no state change
Rejection: 'Reject and keep talking' calls jointChat/rejectClosure; clears proposal banner; optionally posts a system message in the joint chat
Confirmation: calls jointChat/confirmClosure; both parties routed to /cases/:id/closed (reactive route update)
/cases/:id/closed: header shows outcome label (Resolved / Not Resolved / Abandoned) + closure date; closure summary prominently displayed if CLOSED_RESOLVED
Nav tabs: 'Joint Chat' (default, read-only jointMessages), 'My Private Coaching' (read-only privateCoaching/myMessages), 'My Guidance' (synthesisText)
Input and Draft Coach button absent in closed view
Banner: 'This case is closed. No new messages can be added.'
Other party's private coaching tab does NOT exist; URL navigation to other party's private data returns FORBIDDEN

tasks.title: Solo mode UI (PartyToggle, useSoloActingParty hook, all views integrated)
tasks.summary: Implement PartyToggle component and useSoloActingParty hook with URL param state. Integrate into all data queries across private coaching, joint chat, dashboard. Add solo case flagging in dashboard + case header.
acceptance_criteria: PartyToggle: segmented control in coach-accent color; only rendered when case.isSolo=true
Toggle state stored as ?as=initiator|invitee URL query param; browser refresh preserves state
useSoloActingParty(): returns the partyStates row role corresponding to selected party; used as a filter in all queries

privateCoaching/myMessages in solo mode: toggling to Invitee returns only messages linked to the INVITEE partyStates row (same userId, different role); toggling back to Initiator shows only Initiator messages
Toggling parties in solo mode and querying privateCoaching/myMessages returns different message sets (assert count differs or content differs)
Both parties' synthesisTexts in solo mode are different strings (each party receives their own per-party synthesis)
PartyToggle is absent (null rendered) when case.isSolo=false
Solo cases show a 'Solo test' badge on the dashboard case row and in the case PhaseHeader
Solo mode works across all views: private coaching (both party contexts), synthesis display (each party sees their own), joint chat (toggle affects whose perspective is acting), closure flow

tasks.title: Profile page and logout
tasks.summary: Implement /profile: display name edit, sign-out. Sign-out clears client + server session and redirects to /.
acceptance_criteria: /profile shows current user email (read-only) and displayName in an editable text field

users/updateDisplayName mutation (defined in convex/users.ts alongside the upsert logic): input { displayName: string }; calls requireAuth(ctx); updates users.displayName for the current user; returns void. Saving displayName from the profile form calls this mutation; success shows toast 'Display name updated'
'Sign out' calls Convex Auth signOut; client session cleared; user redirected to /
Accessing /profile while logged out redirects to /login
Profile accessible from user avatar/name in top nav

tasks.title: Admin template UI (list, edit with version history, audit log)
tasks.summary: Implement admin-only routes: /admin/templates (TemplatesListPage), /admin/templates/:id (TemplateEditPage with two-pane form + version history), /admin/audit (AuditLogPage). All routes server-side guarded by role=ADMIN check.
acceptance_criteria: GET /admin/templates: 403 page for non-admins (server-side role check, not client flag)
TemplatesListPage: table with Category | Name | Current Version | Status (Active/Archived) | Pinned Cases Count; [+ New Template] button
Pinned Cases Count: computed via a query counting cases where templateVersionId is in the set of versions for that template
'+ New Template': opens form; submission calls admin/templates/create

TemplateEditPage: left pane with category, name, globalGuidance (large textarea), coachInstructions, draftCoachInstructions, notes; 'Publish New Version' primary button creates new immutable version + updates currentVersionId

Version history right pane: each version shows date, admin email, notes, version number, 'View' button; View opens a read-only modal showing both versions' globalGuidance side-by-side for comparison (simple diff: show v(n-1) and v(n) text, no diffing algorithm required in v1)
'Archive Template': confirmation modal shows pinned case count warning; calls admin/templates/archive
AuditLogPage: filterable table (Actor email | Action | Target | Timestamp) newest-first; clicking row opens Drawer with full metadata JSON
Audit log is read-only (no mutations)

tasks.title: Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen reader, reduced motion)
tasks.summary: Systematic accessibility audit and remediation across all 12+ routes per tech spec §5.4 and design doc §7. Covers structural landmarks, aria-live regions on chat UIs, focus management in modals/panels, keyboard shortcuts, WCAG AA contrast for all token combinations in light and dark mode, prefers-reduced-motion, screen reader copy per design doc §7.5.
acceptance_criteria: Every page has exactly one <main> landmark; navigation uses <nav>; side panels use <aside>
All three chat regions (private coaching, joint chat, draft coach) have role='log' and aria-live='polite'
Streaming messages announce 'Coach is replying' exactly once per response, not per token (aria-label on the streaming row, not per character)
PrivacyBanner screen reader text: 'Private conversation. Only you and the AI coach see this.'
Mark-complete CTA aria-label: 'Mark private coaching complete. This moves the case to the joint session with [other party name].'
All modals trap focus (no Tab-out); Esc closes them; focus returns to trigger element on close
Route change moves focus to the page h1 (React Router useEffect on route change)
Draft Coach panel open: focus moves to textarea
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): streaming cursor animation disabled, route crossfades disabled, message fade-ins disabled (all transitions = 0ms)
All text meets 4.5:1 contrast ratio in both light and dark mode (verified via Playwright axe-core accessibility scan in T34)

Keyboard-only critical path test: login → /cases/new → submit form → /cases/:id/private → send message → mark complete → enter joint chat → send message → open Draft Coach → generate draft → send draft → propose closure → confirm closure. User navigates entirely via Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Esc without mouse.
All buttons have visible text label OR aria-label (verified by axe-core: no 'button-name' violations)
Focus rings: --accent color, 2px outline, 2px offset; never removed (no outline: none without a custom focus-visible replacement)

tasks.title: Playwright E2E test suite (all 7 spec files, multi-browser)
tasks.summary: Implement the full Playwright test suite per tech spec §10.1: 7 spec files (auth, solo-full-flow, invite-flow, draft-coach, privacy, admin-templates, state-machine). Configure Playwright projects for chromium, firefox, and webkit. Use CLAUDE_MOCK=true stub. Run solo-full-flow in all three browsers; functional specs in chromium only.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE_MOCK=true: all AI actions use a deterministic stub returning canned responses with configurable delays; no live Anthropic calls in tests
auth.spec.ts: registers via mock magic link, logs out, logs back in; Google OAuth mocked

solo-full-flow.spec.ts: complete mediation end-to-end via solo mode — create case → toggle Initiator → private coaching → mark complete → toggle Invitee → private coaching → mark complete → synthesis visible for both parties → enter joint chat → Draft Coach iteration → 'Draft it for me' → 'Send this message' (verifies message appears in joint chat) → propose closure → confirm → case in Closed section on dashboard. Runs in chromium, firefox, webkit.
invite-flow.spec.ts: two Playwright browser contexts; User A creates case, User B opens invite link in second context, registers, accepts; both see case in their dashboards

draft-coach.spec.ts: open Draft Coach → send messages → click 'Generate Draft' → assert jointMessages count has NOT increased → click 'Send this message' → assert jointMessages count increased by 1; discard a draft → assert no message sent

privacy.spec.ts: as User B, direct Convex query for User A's privateMessages (caseId + userId=A) returns FORBIDDEN; as admin user, same query returns FORBIDDEN; Coach response in joint chat does not contain any 8-token substring from either party's private coaching messages (checked against canned mock responses that include seeded 'other party' text)

admin-templates.spec.ts: admin creates template → publishes v1 → creates case (pinned to v1) → publishes v2 → verifies pinned case still resolves to v1 templateVersionId; archives template → template hidden from picker but pinned case loads normally

state-machine.spec.ts: sendUserMessage when case in BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING → CONFLICT; markComplete twice → idempotent (no error, single privateCoachingCompletedAt value); redeem consumed token → TOKEN_INVALID
Playwright configured with three projects: chromium, firefox, webkit; solo-full-flow and privacy run in all three; other specs run in chromium only (for CI performance)
Playwright axe-core accessibility scan in solo-full-flow.spec.ts for each major page; fails if any WCAG AA critical violation detected
All specs pass 100% with CLAUDE_MOCK=true

tasks.title: Vitest unit tests for core utility modules
tasks.summary: Vitest unit tests per tech spec §10.2 covering privacy filter, prompt assembly, transcript compression, invite token generation, state machine transitions. Can be written and run as soon as T5-T8 are complete — early in the build.
acceptance_criteria: privacyFilter: ≥5 adversarial cases (verbatim quote → passed=false), ≥3 benign cases (rephrased → passed=true), boundary case (exactly 8 tokens → passed=false), below-boundary (7 tokens → passed=true)
prompts.assemblePrompt: for each of 4 roles, asserts system string contains required verbatim instruction segments from §6.3 and the no-quote rule
prompts.assemblePrompt DRAFT_COACH: when otherPartySynthesis is provided in opts, assert it does NOT appear in the assembled messages array

transcriptCompression: over-budget array → smaller array with one SUMMARY message; under-budget → no API call, original array returned; same content compressed twice → only one API call (cache hit verified via spy)
inviteToken generator: 100 generated tokens are all unique, all 32 chars, all url-safe (regex /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/)

stateMachine: all 9 valid transitions succeed; ≥8 illegal transitions (wrong source state) each throw CONFLICT; markComplete idempotency confirmed; decline-invite (DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING → CLOSED_ABANDONED) returns CLOSED_ABANDONED
All tests pass with `npx vitest run`; no live Convex or Anthropic calls

tasks.title: CI pipeline (GitHub Actions: lint, typecheck, unit, E2E)
tasks.summary: Configure .github/workflows/ci.yml with four jobs: lint, typecheck, unit, e2e. All four pass on every PR to main. E2E job seeds a Convex dev deployment with admin user + 3 templates before running Playwright with CLAUDE_MOCK=true and multi-browser projects.
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml defines four jobs: lint, typecheck, unit, e2e; all run on push to PR branches targeting main
lint job: ESLint + Prettier check; fails on any warning or error
typecheck job: tsc --noEmit; fails on any type error
unit job: npx vitest run; all T35 tests pass

e2e job: uses CONVEX_DEPLOY_KEY secret to deploy to a Convex dev environment; runs convex/seed.ts; runs Playwright with CLAUDE_MOCK=true; Playwright projects: chromium (all specs), firefox (solo-full-flow + privacy), webkit (solo-full-flow + privacy)
README documents how to run each check locally: npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e
GitHub Actions workflow caches node_modules for speed
PRD §7.1 launch criterion 7 ('Playwright suite passes 100% in CI') satisfied
T33 and T34 can run in parallel (T36 waits for both before CI passes)
